k_sweep_oracle.description: Oracle ranking: top-k INT8, rest INT4 (block_size=32)
k_sweep_oracle.k_floor_note: k=2 FAIL at 16K (+5.6%), k=3 not validated at 16K/32K, k=4 PASS at all L

interventions.description: K stays INT4, V upgraded to INT8 on sensitive layers
interventions.finding: K quant noise dominates. Even V=INT8 on all 24 layers, K=INT4 gives +20-240%. V protection is useless.
Smaller group size reduces per-layer noise. k=4 PASS@3% at all L. k=0 nearly passes (max +4.5%). Future: combine g=4 with k=2 INT8.
g=8 comparable to g=32 baseline. Not as good as g=4.
g=16 worse than both g=8 and g=32. Non-monotonic interaction with head_dim=64.
Same as Phase 0 oracle k-sweep. Confirms reproducibility.

interventions.description: Post-quant rescale to match clean mean/std per head
interventions.finding: Rescaling destroys position-dependent KV structure. +7-710% at all k.

interventions.description: Clamp K norms to 99th percentile before INT4
interventions.finding: Very slight improvement at k=4 (1.85% vs 1.96%). Does not reduce k*.

winner.improvement_over_baseline: Reduces per-layer INT4 noise by ~8x vs g=32. k=0 nearly passes (max +4.5% vs +429%).
winner.next_step: Combine g=4 with k=2 INT8 to potentially achieve kv_ratio < 0.33 with PASS.
